assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address or Google or Microsoft identity.
No Elastic Cloud organization, trial deployment, or serverless project exists yet.
No solution use case (search, observability, or security) or deployment model is preselected.

developer_goal: Create an Elastic Cloud account and reach the earliest meaningful first-success outcome for a selected Elastic solution.

first_success.outcome: A selected Elastic solution path returns its documented first searchable or console result.
first_success.observable_signal: For the Elasticsearch Console index-and-search route, the expected document appears under hits.hits with its original _source JSON.

route_selection.rule: Select a complete official candidate only after solution and deployment model are fixed; otherwise mark unresolved under the lexicographic route rule.

candidates.name: Elasticsearch Serverless project plus Console index-and-search basics
candidates.reason: Official get-started materials recommend Serverless Elasticsearch for speed, but Observability and Security are peer solutions with different terminals.

candidates.name: Elastic Observability get-started
candidates.reason: Solutions overview presents Observability as an equal recommended path for monitoring and telemetry.

candidates.name: Elastic Security get-started
candidates.reason: Solutions overview presents Security as an equal recommended path for SIEM and threat workflows.

candidates.name: Elastic Cloud Hosted trial deployment
candidates.reason: Trial docs include one hosted deployment alongside three Serverless projects; hosted versus serverless changes required configuration and later steps.

required_path.action: Open Elastic Cloud Sign Up and create an organization with a supported signup method.

required_fields.label: Email and password, or Google or Microsoft identity
required_fields.notes: Docs list email/password, Google, Microsoft, or Marketplace subscribe. Exact public form controls were not fully readable from the registration page fetch.

required_path.observable_result: An Elastic Cloud organization exists and the developer is authenticated under a 14-day trial.

required_path.action: Create the deployment or serverless project type required by the still-unresolved solution route.

required_fields.label: Project or deployment type
required_fields.notes: Serverless requires choosing Elasticsearch, Observability, or Security. Hosted trial uses a different create-deployment flow.

required_path.observable_result: A usable Elastic Cloud resource is available for the selected solution.

required_path.action: Complete the selected solution's first ingest and query or console steps through its documented first-success signal.

required_fields.label: Solution-specific Console or UI inputs
required_fields.notes: Index-and-search basics document Console requests for a books index when that route is chosen; Observability and Security paths differ.

required_path.observable_result: The selected route's documented terminal signal is visible.

prerequisites.description: A supported browser that can reach cloud.elastic.co and related Elastic Cloud domains.

external_gates.description: Google, Microsoft, or Marketplace identity flows, when chosen, require authorization outside Elastic.

unavoidable_waits.description: Elastic must provision the chosen hosted deployment or serverless project before Console or solution UI work can proceed.

platform_outcomes.description: Signing up creates an Elastic Cloud organization and starts a free 14-day trial.
Creating a serverless project or hosted deployment makes Elasticsearch and Kibana endpoints available.

excluded.item: Reading Solutions overview and deployment-options documentation
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence for route selection, not a counted product action.

excluded.item: Subscribing or adding billing to remove trial limits
excluded.reason: Trial signup docs state no credit card is required to start; paid conversion is after the measured first success.

sources.title: Solutions overview
evidence_supported: Three peer solutions: Elasticsearch, Observability, Security
Each solution available as Serverless, Hosted, or self-managed

sources.title: Sign up and create an organization
evidence_supported: Email/password, Google, Microsoft, or Marketplace signup
One hosted deployment and three Serverless projects in trial

sources.title: Create a serverless project
evidence_supported: New-user trial signup to create serverless projects

sources.title: Get started with the Elasticsearch solution/project type
evidence_supported: Choose Elasticsearch project type and name

sources.title: Index and search basics
evidence_supported: Console create index, index documents, and search

sources.title: Elastic Cloud Serverless
evidence_supported: Three serverless solutions: Elasticsearch, Observability, Security

sources.title: Elastic Cloud Trial overview
evidence_supported: Sign up with no credit card required
Create deployment then ingest and explore

uncertainties.question: Which Elastic solution and deployment model define the canonical first-mile journey?
uncertainties.impact: Elasticsearch, Observability, and Security, plus Hosted versus Serverless, change required actions, fields, and the observable first-success signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded developer goal naming one solution and one deployment model, then a complete walkthrough of that route from a fresh trial account.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields appear on the current Elastic Cloud registration form for email versus OAuth signup?
uncertainties.impact: Required-field counts for account creation cannot be finalized without public UI or authenticated walkthrough evidence.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only capture of https://cloud.elastic.co/registration and serverless-registration for each signup method.
